title: Introduction to Node.js and the Express Framework

questions.sentence: Node.js is a runtime environment that allows you to run JavaScript on the server, outside of a web browser.
This enables frontend developers to use their existing JavaScript skills to build full-stack applications.
To make server-side development even simpler, many developers use 'Express.js', a minimalist web framework for Node.js.
Express provides a set of features to handle common backend tasks like routing, which determines how an application responds to a specific URL request.
For example, using Express, you can easily create a route that sends back product information when a user visits '/api/products'.
Understanding Node.js and Express is a significant milestone for any frontend developer looking to understand the complete application lifecycle.
